Class CombinationMessage

java.lang.Object
com.github.mrgarbagegamer.CombinationMessage
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable, Message, StringBuilderFormattable
@AsynchronouslyFormattable public class CombinationMessage extends Object implements Message, StringBuilderFormattable
A specialized Log4j2 Message for logging solution combinations with low overhead.

This class serves as a high-performance logging mechanism for short[] combinations found by the solver. By implementing the Message and StringBuilderFormattable interfaces, it integrates directly with Log4j2's asynchronous logging architecture. This avoids the performance penalties of traditional logging, such as intermediate String creation and formatting overhead on critical application threads.

Performance and Architecture

The primary goal of this class is to enable "garbage-free" logging. It acts as a wrapper for a short[] array, deferring the expensive formatting process to a background I/O thread managed by Log4j2. The formatTo(StringBuilder) method writes the combination data directly into a reusable StringBuilder, preventing object allocation in the application's hot path.

Limitations and Future Improvements

While this class avoids formatting overhead on hot threads, it still allocates a new CombinationMessage instance for each log event. In high-frequency scenarios, this can create GC pressure. Several strategies were considered to mitigate this:

  • Reusable Messages: The most robust solution is to implement Log4j2's ReusableMessage pattern, which would allow instances to be recycled by the framework. This is effective but complex to implement correctly.
  • Object Pooling: A simpler alternative is a custom object pool. However, a shared pool could introduce contention and potential race conditions if managed improperly.
  • Reduced Logging: The simplest option is to log only summary data, but this sacrifices valuable, detailed output.

An alternative to logging altogether is to rely on statistical metrics from the CombinationGeneratorTask, which could provide progress estimates with less performance impact. Setting this up would be more complex, but is a viable option for future exploration.

Thread Safety

This class is not fully thread-safe. The formatting methods are safe for concurrent use, but methods that modify internal state, such as convertTo(Grid.ValueFormat), are not. Instances should be confined to a single thread or properly synchronized if state modifications are required.

Since:
2025.05 - CombinationMessage Introduction
See Also:
  • Logger
  • LogManager
  • LogManager.getLogger()
  • Log4j2 Asynchronous Logging
  • Serialized Form
Performance Characteristics:
O(list.length) for format conversion or string formatting.
Threading Model:
Apart from the convertTo(Grid.ValueFormat) method, which modifies internal state and is not thread-safe, the class is designed to be thread-safe for concurrent read operations.
Memory Management:
Fixed memory footprint after construction; does not allocate except if getFormattedMessage() is called.

  • Field Details

    • list

      private short[] list
      The combination data. The interpretation of this data depends on the format field.
      Since:
      2025.05 - CombinationMessage Introduction
      See Also:
      • getCombination(Grid.ValueFormat)
      • Grid.ValueFormat
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) assignments and retrievals, O(list.length) for format conversion.
      Memory Management:
      Minimal footprint of ~list.length × 2 bytes as a short[].

    • format

      private Grid.ValueFormat format
      The Grid.ValueFormat of the combination stored in list.
      Since:
      2025.05 - CombinationMessage Introduction
      See Also:
      • convertTo(Grid.ValueFormat)
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) assignments and retrievals.
      Memory Management:
      Minimal footprint; enum reference.

  • Constructor Details

    • CombinationMessage

      public CombinationMessage(short[] list, Grid.ValueFormat format)
      Constructs a new CombinationMessage.
      Parameters:
      list - The short[] representing the combination.
      format - The initial Grid.ValueFormat of the combination.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the provided format is Grid.ValueFormat.Bitmask, which is not supported for logging.
      Since:
      2025.05 - CombinationMessage Introduction
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) assignments.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate (except for a new CombinationMessage instance); reuses the passed list.

  • Method Details

    • convertTo

      public void convertTo(Grid.ValueFormat outputFormat)
      Converts the internal combination list to the specified Grid.ValueFormat.

      This method performs an in-place conversion to avoid memory allocation, giving it an O(list.length) time complexity. For greater efficiency, specialized conversion methods (e.g., a dedicated indexToPackedInt()) could be created to minimize conditional checks, though this would increase code complexity.

      Parameters:
      outputFormat - The target Grid.ValueFormat for the conversion.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if conversion to Grid.ValueFormat.Bitmask is attempted.
      Since:
      2025.07 - Cell Format Support
      See Also:
      • Grid.indexToPacked(short)
      • Grid.packedToIndex(short)
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(list.length) format conversion.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe, as it mutates internal state.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate; performs in-place conversion of list.

    • formatTo

      public void formatTo(StringBuilder buffer)
      Formats the combination as a human-readable string into the provided StringBuilder.

      This is the core method for garbage-free logging. It ensures the combination is in a readable Grid.ValueFormat.PackedInt format and appends it to the buffer. Values are zero-padded for better readability.

      Specified by:
      formatTo in interface StringBuilderFormattable
      Parameters:
      buffer - The StringBuilder to which the formatted message will be appended.
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - (implicitly) if the provided buffer is null.
      Since:
      2025.05 - CombinationMessage Introduction
      See Also:
      • StringBuilderFormattable
      • StringBuilderFormattable.formatTo(StringBuilder)
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(list.length) iteration through the list.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe, unless format conversion is triggered.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate; appends directly to the provided StringBuilder.

    • getFormattedMessage

      public String getFormattedMessage()
      Returns the formatted message as a String.

      This method serves as a fallback and is not typically called by Log4j2's asynchronous appenders. It necessarily allocates a new String, defeating the purpose of a garbage-free message.

      Specified by:
      getFormattedMessage in interface Message
      Returns:
      The formatted message as a new String.
      Since:
      2025.05 - CombinationMessage Introduction
      See Also:
      • StringBuilder.toString()
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) call to formatTo(StringBuilder)
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe, unless conversion is triggered.
      Memory Management:
      Allocates a new String for the return value, but avoids intermediate String allocations.

    • getCombination

      public short[] getCombination(Grid.ValueFormat outputFormat)
      Returns the raw combination array, converting it to the specified format if necessary.

      The current design prioritizes simplicity, but for optimal performance, creating specialized methods for each format conversion would reduce the overhead of conditional checks.

      Parameters:
      outputFormat - The desired Grid.ValueFormat for the returned array.
      Returns:
      The short[] combination in the specified format.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if conversion to Grid.ValueFormat.Bitmask is attempted.
      Since:
      2025.05 - CombinationMessage Introduction
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(list.length) if conversion is needed; O(1) otherwise.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe, unless conversion is triggered.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate; returns the internal array, converting in-place if needed.

Performance-optimized brute-force solver for hexagonal grid Lights Out puzzles
